UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user account or an organization's profile within the CleanOps Hub, acting as a container for their specific sites, cleaners, and schedules.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

name.description: The name associated with the user profile, such as a company name or the user's full name.

email.description: The email address for identifying the user profile.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was last updated.

Site.title: Site
Site.description: Represents a physical location or area that requires cleaning services, managed by a specific user profile.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Site entity.

name.description: The name of the cleaning site (e.g., "Anne Mclaren", "Cedar").

status.description: The current operational status of the site, indicating client satisfaction or issues (e.g., "Client happy", "Operations request", "Client concerns", "Site under action plan").

userProfileId.description: Reference to the UserProfile that owns and manages this site. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Site)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the site was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the site was last updated.

Cleaner.title: Cleaner
Cleaner.description: Represents an individual cleaner, including their personal details and performance rating, managed by a specific user profile.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Cleaner entity.

name.description: The full name of the cleaner.

performanceRating.description: The performance rating of the cleaner, based on feedback or operational assessment (e.g., "Excellent feedback", "Site satisfied", "Needs retraining").

userProfileId.description: Reference to the UserProfile that manages this cleaner. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Cleaner)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the cleaner profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the cleaner profile was last updated.

CleaningScheduleEntry.title: CleaningScheduleEntry
CleaningScheduleEntry.description: Represents a single entry in the cleaning schedule, linking a specific site to a cleaner with designated start and end times for a user profile.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CleaningScheduleEntry entity.

siteId.description: Reference to the Site entity scheduled for cleaning. (Relationship: Site 1:N CleaningScheduleEntry)

cleanerId.description: Reference to the Cleaner entity assigned to perform the cleaning task at the site. (Relationship: Cleaner 1:N CleaningScheduleEntry)

startTime.description: The scheduled start time for the cleaning task (e.g., "23:30").

endTime.description: The scheduled end time for the cleaning task (e.g., "01:45").

userProfileId.description: Reference to the UserProfile that owns and manages this schedule entry. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N CleaningScheduleEntry)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the schedule entry was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the schedule entry was last updated.

definition.description: Top-level collection for user or organization profiles. Each document represents a distinct operational hub. It includes a `members` map for collaborative authorization, where keys are Firebase UIDs and values are roles (e.g., 'owner', 'admin', 'viewer'). The document ID `{userProfileId}` is a unique, generated identifier for the profile.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific UserProfile document.

definition.description: Subcollection storing physical cleaning sites, each belonging to a specific UserProfile. Documents in this collection include the denormalized `userProfileId` and a denormalized `members` map from the parent UserProfile for authorization independence.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent UserProfile.

params.name: siteId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific Site document within a UserProfile.

definition.description: Subcollection storing cleaner profiles, each managed by a specific UserProfile. Documents in this collection include the denormalized `userProfileId` and a denormalized `members` map from the parent UserProfile for authorization independence.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent UserProfile.

params.name: cleanerId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific Cleaner document within a UserProfile.

definition.description: Subcollection storing individual entries in the cleaning schedule, each belonging to a specific UserProfile. Documents in this collection include the denormalized `userProfileId`, `siteId`, `cleanerId`, and a denormalized `members` map from the parent UserProfile for authorization independence.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent UserProfile.

params.name: scheduleEntryId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific CleaningScheduleEntry document within a UserProfile.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure is designed to support a multi-tenant application where each 'UserProfile' acts as an independent operational hub, owning its own sites, cleaners, and schedules. The core strategy hinges on structural segregation and authorization independence through denormalization.

1.  **Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** To eliminate the need for `get()` calls in security rules, which break atomic operations and complicate debugging, the authorization context (specifically, the `members` map from the `UserProfile`) is denormalized into all subcollection documents (`Site`, `Cleaner`, `CleaningScheduleEntry`). This means each site, cleaner, and schedule entry document will contain a copy of the `members` map from its owning UserProfile. This allows security rules to evaluate permissions directly on the document being accessed, ensuring atomic writes and simpler, more predictable rule evaluation. A Cloud Function would be responsible for updating these denormalized `members` maps in subcollection documents when the parent `UserProfile`'s `members` change.

2.  **Structural Segregation (Homogeneous Security Posture):** All entities belonging to a specific UserProfile (Sites, Cleaners, Cleaning Schedule Entries) are grouped under their respective `userProfileId` in a hierarchical path (e.g., `/userProfiles/{userProfileId}/sites/{siteId}`). This ensures that all documents within a given subcollection share the same security requirements tied to their parent `UserProfile`'s membership.

3.  **Access Modeling & QAPs (Queries as Permissions):**
    *   **UserProfiles:** The `/userProfiles/{userProfileId}` collection uses a 'membership map' (`members: { [uid]: 'role' }`) within each `UserProfile` document itself. This allows multiple Firebase authenticated users (`request.auth.uid`) to be associated with and manage a single `UserProfile`. To list profiles a user has access to, a query like `db.collection('userProfiles').where('members.' + auth.uid, 'exists')` can be used. This directly enforces that only profiles where the user is a member are returned, satisfying QAPs for `list` operations on the top-level entity.
    *   **Subcollections (Sites, Cleaners, CleaningScheduleEntries):** For subcollections like `/userProfiles/{userProfileId}/sites/{siteId}`, the `userProfileId` in the path and the denormalized `members` map in each document facilitate secure and efficient `list` operations. For example, to list all sites for a specific `userProfileId` that the authenticated user has access to, a query would look like `db.collection('userProfiles').doc(userProfileId).collection('sites').where('members.' + auth.uid, 'in', ['owner', 'admin', 'viewer'])`. This combination of path-based scoping and denormalized permissions ensures secure `list` queries without complex rule dependencies or inefficient cross-document reads.

4.  **Data Clarity and Predictability:** Consistent path naming (`userProfiles`, `{userProfileId}`, `sites`, etc.) and the explicit `userProfileId` field within child entities (which aligns with the path structure) enhance clarity. The `status` field in `Site` and `performanceRating` in `Cleaner` explicitly model state, rather than inferring it, simplifying data interpretation and rule writing.

This design prioritizes robust security, simplified rule sets, and efficient querying for a multi-tenant application, while explicitly addressing the needs for authorization independence and QAPs.
